title: Contact
text:
I’m always interested in connecting with professionals in data analytics, supply chain, logistics operations, or business intelligence.



Email: oscardaraquintero@gmail.com
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/oscar-quintero-40038489
GitHub: github.com/Caelusbellum
Location: Richmond, VA





Feel free to reach out if you’re working on interesting projects at the intersection of logistics/supply chain and data analytics. I’m particularly interested in roles that allow me to combine operational leadership with modern analytical tools.

Thank you for visiting!

title: About Me
text:
Data-Driven Logistics & Operations Leader
Richmond, VA | Expected Graduation: September 2026
I am a results-oriented logistics and operations professional with more than 20 years of experience turning complex financial and movement data into clear, actionable decisions for senior leaders. Across roles in the United States, Europe, and the Middle East, I have managed budgets up to $296 million, overseen large-scale deployment and training operations, and built automated reporting tools that dramatically improved analysis speed and accuracy.
I am currently pursuing a Master of Science in Applied Business Analytics at Boston University Metropolitan College. This program is allowing me to bridge my extensive operational and supply chain experience with modern data science, machine learning, and business intelligence tools. My goal is to combine real-world leadership and logistical expertise with advanced analytical capabilities to drive measurable improvements in organizational performance.



Deep expertise in resource management, budget execution, and strategic logistics
Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams in complex, high-stakes environments
Growing technical proficiency in Python, SQL, Pandas, NumPy, and automated analytics
Bilingual (English & Spanish) with strong communication and team unification skills
A track record of earning trust and delivering results even in challenging conditions

I am particularly interested in roles that allow me to blend hands-on analytics with real-world operational challenges — helping organizations make smarter decisions about resources, risk, and efficiency.





M.S. Applied Business Analytics, Boston University Metropolitan College (Expected Sep 2026)
Advanced Operations Course, U.S. Army Command and General Staff College (2021)
Intermediate Level Education, U.S. Army Command and General Staff College (2014)
B.A. Spanish / Linguistics, Texas Tech University (2001)


Ready to connect? Contact me

title: Projects
text:
Here are some of the key projects that highlight my ability to combine operational leadership with data analytics and process improvement.


Role: Lead Developer & Project Manager
Tools: Python, Pandas, Excel + Power Query, Automated Scheduling
Built an automated reporting system that replaced manual Excel processes for tracking a $296M budget. The tool pulled data from multiple sources, cleaned and standardized it, then generated executive-ready reports.

Reduced report generation time from 3–4 days to under 2 hours
Improved data accuracy by approximately 80%
Used by senior leadership for monthly resource allocation decisions

Technologies: Python, Pandas, NumPy, Jupyter, automated Python scripts




Role: Mission Support & Logistics Exercise Planner
Tools: Data analysis, scheduling optimization, stakeholder coordination
Led the planning and execution of large-scale joint training exercises involving multiple countries and military branches. Developed analytical tools to optimize resource allocation and scheduling.

Coordinated resources for exercises involving hundreds of personnel
Created dashboards to track readiness metrics and identify bottlenecks
Improved deployment efficiency through better data visibility

Technologies: SQL, Excel advanced modeling, data visualization




Role: Graduate Analytics Student
Tools: Python, SQL, Polars, Jupyter Notebooks
Current capstone-style project focused on applying modern data analytics to real-world logistics and supply chain challenges as part of the Applied Business Analytics program.
Technologies: Python, SQL, Pandas/Polars, Data Visualization





Resource integration and budget execution systems for U.S. Army Europe-Africa
Real-time operational tracking tools used during NATO exercises
Team performance and readiness analytics during multi-year overseas assignments


Want to see the code or reports?
Many of my projects are available on GitHub or available upon request.

title: Oscar Quintero
text:
Turning complex operational data into clear strategic decisions.
20+ years leading logistics, resource management, and large-scale operations across the U.S., Europe, and the Middle East.
Currently pursuing a Master of Science in Applied Business Analytics at Boston University (expected Sep 2026).

title: Oscar Quintero
text:
Welcome
I am a results-oriented logistics and operations professional with extensive experience managing multimillion-dollar budgets, complex deployments, and cross-functional teams. I specialize in transforming raw operational and financial data into actionable insights that drive efficiency and reduce risk.
My background combines deep real-world operational leadership with growing expertise in modern data analytics, Python, SQL, and business intelligence tools. I’m particularly passionate about bridging traditional supply chain management with advanced analytics and AI to solve complex organizational challenges.

title: Oscar Quintero
text:
Quick Highlights

Budget & Resource Management: Managed budgets exceeding $296 million
Analytics Focus: Building expertise in Python, SQL, Pandas, and automated reporting
Leadership: Led teams across multiple continents in high-stakes environments
Bilingual: English & Spanish








Currently seeking opportunities in Data Analytics, Supply Chain Analytics, or Logistics Operations roles (especially in Virginia, Alabama, or Texas).
